- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""
2	Section-by-section report writer for polaris graph.
3	
4	Each section is a separate LLM call, keeping output under 800 words
5	to stay within the LLM's quality sweet spot.
6	
7	No single mega-call. No CoT leakage. No post-processing.
8	"""
9	
10	import asyncio
11	import logging
12	import os
13	import re
14	from typing import Optional
15	
16	import numpy as np
17	
18	from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
19	from src.polaris_graph.schemas import (
20	    QuestionDecomposition,
21	    ReportOutline,
22	    SectionDraft,
23	    SectionOutlineItem,
24	)
25	from src.polaris_graph.state import (
26	    EvidencePiece,
27	    MAX_WORDS_PER_SECTION,
28	    PG_SECTION_CONTINUATION_MAX_TOKENS,
29	    PG_SECTION_EVIDENCE_TOP_K,
30	    PG_SECTION_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS,
31	    PG_SYNTHESIS_STRUCTURED_MAX_TOKENS,
32	    ReportSection,
33	)
34	from src.polaris_graph.tracing import get_tracer
35	
36	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
37	
38	SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are writing one section of a research report. You have {n_evidence} evidence pieces.
39	
40	GENERATION RULE: Write ONLY from the evidence provided. For every factual claim, cite
41	the evidence ID immediately after: [CITE:evidence_id]. If you cannot cite a claim, do not write it.
42	
43	STRUCTURE RULES:
44	1. If your evidence contains 4+ comparable data points (same measurement, different entities),
45	   present them as a MARKDOWN TABLE with citations in each cell.
46	2. If your evidence contains time-series data or trends, describe them quantitatively.
47	3. End each section with a **Key Findings** subsection: 3-5 cited bullet points summarizing
48	   the claims a reader should remember from this section.
49	4. Write analytically: comparisons, trade-offs, implications — not just facts.
50	
51	CITATION RULES:
52	5. Every factual claim MUST have [CITE:evidence_id] immediately after.
53	6. Include exact numbers, dates, measurements from evidence. NEVER invent numbers.
54	7. STRONGLY prefer evidence marked [VERIFIED] over [UNVERIFIED].
55	8. Do NOT cite the same source more than 3 times per section. Spread across sources.
56	9. If you CANNOT cite a number, use qualitative language ("evidence suggests") instead.
57	10. Use EXACTLY the units from the source. Never convert or paraphrase units.
58	
59	FIX-HALLUC-1 — ABSOLUTE HALLUCINATION BAN (read carefully):
60	  Use ONLY the evidence provided above. Do NOT use training knowledge, do NOT recall
61	  studies, papers, PMIDs, author names, sample sizes, regulatory bodies, or quoted
62	  expert reactions unless the specific fact is supported by an evidence piece shown
63	  to you AND tagged with [CITE:evidence_id]. If you feel tempted to write a specific
64	  fact you "know" but it is not in the evidence: DROP IT. Under NO circumstance invent
65	  or recall: PMIDs, DOIs, author names (e.g., "Zhong et al."), sample sizes (e.g., "20,078 adults"),
66	  follow-up durations, percent risk ratios, study locations, cohort names, regulatory agency
67	  positions (FDA/EFSA/EMA/FTC/Health Canada), media outlets (e.g., "Science Media Centre"),
68	  or expert critiques. If the prose needs such a fact and no evidence supports it, write a
69	  neutral qualitative sentence instead (e.g., "observational data have raised long-term
70	  safety questions") without specifics. Sections containing uncited specific numbers or
71	  uncited named entities WILL BE REJECTED by the audit.
72	
73	FIX-6 — INTERPRETIVE OVERLAY BAN:
74	  Uncited sentences are allowed ONLY as neutral connectives between two
75	  [CITE:evidence_id]-anchored sentences. The following are FORBIDDEN without a
76	  supporting [CITE:]:
77	    * Clinical recommendations: "clinical translation should...", "clinicians
78	      should therefore...", "practitioners are advised to..."
79	    * Qualifier overlays: "the most robust signal is...", "the most important
80	      finding is...", "the best-studied protocol is..."
81	    * Synthesis overlays: "taken together...", "integrating these threads
82	      suggests...", "the evidence supports an X framing rather than Y..."
83	    * Editorial framings: "this framing provides public-health context but...",
84	      "adherence is the first-order mechanism...", "research priorities are..."
85	    * Fabricated taxonomies not attributable to a specific cited source.
86	  If you cannot cite a claim, do NOT write it. "Taken together" is a banned
87	  sentence opener unless the sentence that follows carries a [CITE:].
88	
89	FIX-6 — TITLE-ONLY BAN:
90	  Do NOT pad sections with standalone sentences that only restate a source's
91	  title or scope (e.g. "A review of X examines whether Y [CITE:ev_xxx]"). Every
92	  sentence must report a specific finding, number, or causal link — not merely
93	  that a source exists. Thin atomic facts must be integrated into comparative
94	  paragraphs or dropped.
95	
96	FIX-6 — CHERRY-PICK PREVENTION:
97	  When citing a meta-analysis or review with multiple pooled outcomes, either
98	  (a) report all primary outcomes the source analyzed, or (b) note explicitly
99	  that you are reporting one outcome among several. Do NOT present a single
100	  statistically significant finding from a predominantly-null study as if it
101	  were the headline result.
102	
103	FORWARD-PROMISE RULE: Do NOT enumerate topics the report "will cover" unless you will
104	  actually cover them in this section. Do not introduce axes, frameworks, or themes that
105	  require content beyond what your evidence supports.
106	
107	STYLE RULES:
108	11. Write in third person, academic register. No meta-commentary or preamble.
109	12. Use hedging words (may, might, could) ONLY for genuinely uncertain findings. Max 5 per section.
110	13. Use transition words SPARINGLY — max 1 per 200 words. NEVER start consecutive sentences with them.
111	14. Do NOT include chain-of-thought, reasoning, or planning text.
112	15. Do NOT repeat the section title in the content.
113	16. Do NOT repeat statistics that appeared in previous sections.
114	17. When citing regulatory thresholds, note when they were established.
115	
116	LENGTH: Write proportional to evidence depth. {n_evidence} pieces ~ {suggested_words} words.
117	Do NOT pad beyond what evidence supports. Quality over quantity."""
118	
119	OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a research report architect. Design report structure FROM evidence, not templates.
120	
121	Given evidence clusters with viability assessments, create a data-driven outline where:
122	- Each section EXISTS because evidence supports it (not because a template demands it)
123	- Section depth is PROPORTIONAL to evidence depth (more evidence = more detailed section)
124	- Clusters with contradictory evidence get balanced treatment sections
125	- Clusters assessed as BRIEF become bullet points in an "Additional Findings" section
126	
127	Requirements:
128	1. Each section should have a clear, specific title (not generic like "Introduction").
129	2. Sections should flow logically from background to findings to implications.
130	3. Evidence will be assigned to sections automatically — focus on designing the best structure.
131	4. Each section's target_words should be proportional to its evidence: min(200 + n_evidence * 80, 2000).
132	5. Include a brief abstract (150-250 words) summarizing key findings.
133	6. For each section, state which cluster numbers it draws from.
134	7. No two sections should cover the same subtopic. Each section must have a unique analytical angle.
135	8. The abstract should NOT include specific numeric claims about source counts or evidence quantities.
136	9. FIX-MP18: The abstract MUST include: (a) scope, (b) exclusions, (c) evidence limitations.
137	10. If evidence contains comparable data points, note which sections should include TABLES.
138	11. Do NOT pad. A section with 3 evidence pieces warrants a concise paragraph, not 800 words.
139	
140	Output format example:
141	{"title": "Comprehensive Analysis of X: Mechanisms, Impacts, and Regulatory Landscape", "abstract": "This report examines...", "sections": [{"section_id": "s01", "title": "Historical Context and Emergence of X", "description": "Traces the discovery and early research. Covers clusters 1, 2. Contains comparison data suitable for TABLE.", "evidence_ids": [], "target_words": 600, "order": 1}], "total_target_words": 6000}"""
142	
143	
144	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
145	# Phase 5: Modular Prompt Fragments (Claw Code 160+ fragment architecture)
146	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
147	
148	_PROMPT_FRAGMENT_DIR = os.path.join(
149	    os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))),
150	    "config", "prompts",
151	)
152	
153	
154	def _detect_section_type(evidence_items: list[dict]) -> str:
155	    """Detect section type from evidence CHARACTERISTICS, not title.
156	
157	    Returns one of: comparison, safety, mechanism, methodology, clinical (default).
158	    """
159	    if not evidence_items:
160	        return "clinical"
161	
162	    statements = " ".join(
163	        e.get("statement", "") for e in evidence_items[:20]
164	    ).lower()
165	
166	    # Count keyword signals per type
167	    comparison_kw = sum(1 for kw in [
168	        "compared to", "versus", "vs ", "outperformed", "superior",
169	        "inferior", "non-inferior", "effect size", "odds ratio",
170	        "relative risk", "hazard ratio", "confidence interval",
171	    ] if kw in statements)
172	
173	    safety_kw = sum(1 for kw in [
174	        "adverse", "risk", "contraindication", "side effect", "toxicity",
175	        "mortality", "morbidity", "withdrawal", "discontinuation",
176	        "safety", "harm", "dangerous", "fatal",
177	    ] if kw in statements)
178	
179	    mechanism_kw = sum(1 for kw in [
180	        "mechanism", "pathway", "receptor", "enzyme", "autophagy",
181	        "signaling", "metabolic", "molecular", "cellular", "binding",
182	        "expression", "phosphorylation", "inhibition", "activation",
183	    ] if kw in statements)
184	
185	    methodology_kw = sum(1 for kw in [
186	        "meta-analysis", "systematic review", "grade", "bias",
187	        "heterogeneity", "sample size", "power", "blinding",
188	        "randomization", "funnel plot", "i-squared", "cochrane",
189	    ] if kw in statements)
190	
191	    scores = {
192	        "comparison": comparison_kw,
193	        "safety": safety_kw,
194	        "mechanism": mechanism_kw,
195	        "methodology": methodology_kw,
196	    }
197	
198	    best_type = max(scores, key=scores.get)
199	    if scores[best_type] >= 2:
200	        return best_type
201	    return "clinical"
202	
203	
204	def _load_prompt_fragment(section_type: str) -> str:
205	    """Load a prompt fragment for the detected section type.
206	
207	    Returns fragment text, or empty string if file not found.
208	    Max 300 tokens (~1200 chars) enforced.
209	    """
210	    _max_chars = int(os.getenv("PG_PROMPT_FRAGMENT_MAX_CHARS", "1200"))
211	    fragment_path = os.path.join(_PROMPT_FRAGMENT_DIR, f"fragment_{section_type}.md")
212	
213	    try:
214	        with open(fragment_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
215	            content = f.read().strip()
216	        if len(content) > _max_chars:
217	            content = content[:_max_chars].rsplit("\n", 1)[0]
218	        return content
219	    except FileNotFoundError:
220	        logger.debug(
221	            "[polaris graph] Phase 5: Fragment file not found: %s",
222	            fragment_path,
223	        )
224	        return ""
225	    except Exception as exc:
226	        logger.warning(
227	            "[polaris graph] Phase 5: Failed to load fragment '%s': %s",
228	            section_type, str(exc)[:200],
229	        )
230	        return ""
231	
232	
233	def _load_base_rules() -> str:
234	    """Load the base rules prompt fragment."""
235	    base_path = os.path.join(_PROMPT_FRAGMENT_DIR, "base_rules.md")
236	    try:
237	        with open(base_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
238	            return f.read().strip()
239	    except Exception:
240	        return ""
241	
242	
243	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
244	# FIX-107I: Per-section evidence filtering via embedding similarity
245	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
246	
247	def _filter_evidence_for_section(
248	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece],
249	    section_title: str,
250	    section_description: str,
251	    top_k: int = 100,
252	) -> list[EvidencePiece]:
253	    """FIX-107I: Filter evidence by embedding similarity to section topic.
254	
255	    Before writing each section, compute cosine similarity between the
256	    section topic (title + description) and every evidence statement.
257	    Return the top_k most similar pieces so the LLM receives focused,
258	    relevant evidence instead of the full pool.
259	
260	    Graceful fallback: if embedding fails for any reason, returns the
261	    full evidence list unchanged (no silent degradation of capability,
262	    just skips the optimization).
263	
264	    Args:
265	        evidence: Full pool of evidence pieces for the section (already
266	            filtered to outline-assigned IDs).
267	        section_title: The section's title string.
268	        section_description: The section's description string.
269	        top_k: Maximum number of evidence pieces to return.
270	
271	    Returns:
272	        Filtered list of at most ``top_k`` evidence pieces, sorted by
273	        descending similarity to the section topic.
274	    """
275	    # If evidence pool is already small enough, skip embedding entirely
276	    if len(evidence) <= top_k:
277	        logger.debug(
278	            "[polaris graph] FIX-107I: Section '%s' has %d evidence (<= top_k=%d), "
279	            "skipping filtering",
280	            section_title[:50],
281	            len(evidence),
282	            top_k,
283	        )
284	        return evidence
285	
286	    try:
287	        from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_text, embed_texts
288	    except ImportError:
289	        logger.warning(
290	            "[polaris graph] FIX-107I: embedding_service unavailable, "
291	            "skipping per-section evidence filtering for '%s'",
292	            section_title[:50],
293	        )
294	        return evidence
295	
296	    try:
297	        # Build query from section title + description
298	        query_text = f"{section_title}. {section_description}"
299	        query_vec = np.array(embed_text(query_text))
300	
301	        # Batch-embed all evidence statements
302	        statements = [e.get("statement", "") for e in evidence]
303	        statement_vecs = np.array(embed_texts(statements))
304	
305	        # Cosine similarity (embeddings are pre-normalized -> dot product)
306	        similarities = statement_vecs @ query_vec
307	
308	        # Get indices of top_k most similar evidence pieces
309	        # np.argpartition is O(n) vs O(n log n) for argsort, but we need
310	        # sorted output for deterministic ordering, so use argsort on the
311	        # partitioned subset.
312	        if len(similarities) > top_k:
313	            top_indices = np.argsort(similarities)[-top_k:][::-1]
314	        else:
315	            top_indices = np.argsort(similarities)[::-1]
316	
317	        filtered = [evidence[i] for i in top_indices]
318	
319	        # Log the filtering result
320	        kept_ids = [e.get("evidence_id", "?") for e in filtered]
321	        min_sim = float(similarities[top_indices[-1]]) if len(top_indices) > 0 else 0.0
322	        max_sim = float(similarities[top_indices[0]]) if len(top_indices) > 0 else 0.0
323	
324	        logger.info(
325	            "[polaris graph] FIX-107I: Section '%s' filtered %d -> %d evidence pieces "
326	            "(sim range %.3f-%.3f)",
327	            section_title[:50],
328	            len(evidence),
329	            len(filtered),
330	            min_sim,
331	            max_sim,
332	        )
333	
334	        return filtered
335	
336	    except Exception as exc:
337	        # Graceful fallback: return all evidence if embedding fails
338	        logger.warning(
339	            "[polaris graph] FIX-107I: Embedding failed for section '%s': %s — "
340	            "returning all %d evidence pieces unfiltered",
341	            section_title[:50],
342	            str(exc)[:200],
343	            len(evidence),
344	        )
345	        return evidence
346	
347	
348	def _build_comparison_tables(
349	    section_evidence: list[dict],
350	) -> str:
351	    """RC-6: Build pre-formatted markdown tables from comparable_metrics.
352	
353	    Groups metrics by metric_name across evidence pieces. For groups with
354	    3+ data points, generates markdown tables with [CITE:evidence_id] in
355	    the Source column.
356	
357	    Returns formatted table block string, or empty string if no tables.
358	    """
359	    from collections import defaultdict
360	
361	    # Collect all comparable_metrics from evidence cards
362	    metric_groups: dict[str, list[tuple[dict, str]]] = defaultdict(list)
363	    for ev in section_evidence:
364	        metrics = ev.get("comparable_metrics") or []
365	        ev_id = ev.get("evidence_id", "?")
366	        for m in metrics:
367	            if isinstance(m, dict) and m.get("metric_name") and m.get("value") is not None:
368	                metric_groups[m["metric_name"]].append((m, ev_id))
369	
370	    if not metric_groups:
371	        return ""
372	
373	    tables = []
374	    for metric_name, entries in sorted(metric_groups.items()):
375	        if len(entries) < 3:
376	            continue
377	
378	        # Build markdown table
379	        table_lines = [
380	            f"\n**Comparison: {metric_name.replace('_', ' ').title()}**\n",
381	            "| Source | Entity | Value | Unit | Condition |",
382	            "|--------|--------|-------|------|-----------|",
383	        ]
384	        for m, ev_id in entries[:15]:  # Cap at 15 rows
385	            entity = m.get("entity", "")
386	            value = m.get("value", "")
387	            unit = m.get("unit", "")
388	            condition = m.get("condition", "")
389	            table_lines.append(f"| [CITE:{ev_id}] | {entity} | {value} | {unit} | {condition} |")
390	
391	        tables.append("\n".join(table_lines))
392	
393	    if not tables:
394	        return ""
395	
396	    return "\n\n".join(tables)
397	
398	
399	def _format_conflicts_for_prompt(
400	    evidence_conflicts: list[dict],
401	    section_evidence_ids: list[str],
402	) -> str:
403	    """FIX-ENV4: Format evidence conflicts relevant to a section for inclusion in prompts.
404	
405	    Filters the global conflict list to only those conflicts where at least
406	    one evidence piece is assigned to this section. Returns a formatted
407	    string block for injection into LLM prompts.
408	
409	    Args:
410	        evidence_conflicts: Full list of detected conflict dicts.
411	        section_evidence_ids: Evidence IDs assigned to this section.
412	
413	    Returns:
414	        Formatted conflict summary string, or empty string if no relevant conflicts.
415	    """
416	    if not evidence_conflicts or not section_evidence_ids:
417	        return ""
418

460	    """Build a compact evidence summary for question decomposition (RC-3).
461

466	    gold = sum(1 for e in evidence if e.get("quality_tier") == "GOLD")
467	    silver = sum(1 for e in evidence if e.get("quality_tier") == "SILVER")
468	    lines.append(f"Quality: {gold} GOLD, {silver} SILVER, {len(evidence) - gold - silver} BRONZE")

473	        perspectives[p] = perspectives.get(p, 0) + 1
474	    if perspectives:

476	            f"{k} ({v})" for k, v in sorted(perspectives.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:6]

480	        theme = cl.get("theme", cl.get("label", f"Cluster {i}"))
481	        count = len(cl.get("evidence_ids", []))
482	        lines.append(f"  Cluster {i}: {theme} ({count} evidence)")

484	    return "\n".join(lines)

764	def _assign_evidence_to_sections(
765	    outline: ReportOutline,
766	    clusters: list[dict],
767	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece],
768	) -> ReportOutline:
769	    """FIX-OUTLINE: Algorithmically assign evidence from clusters to outline sections.
770	
771	    Uses word overlap (Jaccard similarity) between section title+description
772	    and cluster theme+description to find the best cluster->section mapping.
773	    Each cluster's evidence_ids are assigned to its best-matching section.
774	
775	    This replaces LLM-based evidence assignment, which was the root cause of
776	    outline timeouts (forcing the LLM to assign 900+ evidence IDs to sections
777	    within a structured JSON schema call).
778	    """
779	    if not clusters or not outline.sections:
780	        return outline
781	
782	    sorted_sections = sorted(outline.sections, key=lambda s: s.order)
783	
784	    def _word_set(text: str) -> set[str]:
785	        """Extract meaningful words (>2 chars) as a lowercase set."""
786	        return {w.lower() for w in text.split() if len(w) > 2}
787	
788	    # Build cluster word sets from theme + description
789	    cluster_word_sets = [
790	        _word_set(f"{c.get('theme', '')} {c.get('description', '')}")
791	        for c in clusters
792	    ]
793	
794	    # Build section word sets from title + description
795	    section_word_sets = [
796	        _word_set(f"{s.title} {s.description}")
797	        for s in sorted_sections
798	    ]
799	
800	    # Assign each cluster to its best-matching section
801	    section_evidence: dict[int, list[str]] = {
802	        i: [] for i in range(len(sorted_sections))
803	    }
804	    assigned_evidence_ids: set[str] = set()
805	
806	    for ci, cluster in enumerate(clusters):
807	        c_words = cluster_word_sets[ci]
808	        best_section_idx = 0
809	        best_similarity = -1.0
810	
811	        for si, s_words in enumerate(section_word_sets):
812	            if not c_words or not s_words:
813	                continue
814	            intersection = len(c_words & s_words)
815	            union = len(c_words | s_words)
816	            similarity = intersection / union if union > 0 else 0.0
817	            if similarity > best_similarity:
818	                best_similarity = similarity
819	                best_section_idx = si
820	
821	        ev_ids = cluster.get("evidence_ids", [])
822	        section_evidence[best_section_idx].extend(ev_ids)
823	        assigned_evidence_ids.update(ev_ids)
824	
825	    # FIX-MP6: Assign unassigned evidence to section with lowest count + any overlap.
826	    # This distributes evidence more evenly (prevents piling on large sections).
827	    all_evidence_ids = {
828	        e.get("evidence_id", "") for e in evidence if e.get("evidence_id")
829	    }
830	    unassigned = all_evidence_ids - assigned_evidence_ids
831	
832	    if unassigned:
833	        ev_map = {e.get("evidence_id", ""): e for e in evidence}
834	        for eid in unassigned:
835	            ev = ev_map.get(eid)
836	            if not ev:
837	                continue
838	            stmt_words = _word_set(ev.get("statement", ""))
839	            if not stmt_words:
840	                # No words to match — assign to section with fewest evidence
841	                min_idx = min(section_evidence, key=lambda k: len(section_evidence[k]))
842	                section_evidence[min_idx].append(eid)
843	                continue
844	
845	            # Find all sections with ANY word overlap, pick the one with fewest evidence
846	            candidates: list[tuple[int, int]] = []  # (section_idx, count)
847	            for si, s_words in enumerate(section_word_sets):
848	                if s_words and (stmt_words & s_words):
849	                    candidates.append((si, len(section_evidence[si])))
850	
851	            if candidates:
852	                # Pick section with lowest evidence count (ties: first match)
853	                best_idx = min(candidates, key=lambda t: t[1])[0]
854	            else:
855	                # No overlap at all — fall back to section with fewest evidence
856	                best_idx = min(section_evidence, key=lambda k: len(section_evidence[k]))
857	
858	            section_evidence[best_idx].append(eid)
859	
860	        logger.info(
861	            "[polaris graph] FIX-MP6: Redistributed %d unassigned evidence pieces "
862	            "to lowest-count sections with word overlap",
863	            len(unassigned),
864	        )
865	
866	    # FIX-CITE-DIV-1: Cap any single source at max_source_pct of section evidence
867	    max_source_pct = float(os.getenv("PG_MAX_SOURCE_PCT_PER_SECTION", "0.33"))
868	    ev_map_for_div = {e.get("evidence_id", ""): e for e in evidence}
869	    diversity_trimmed = 0
870	    for si in section_evidence:
871	        ids = section_evidence[si]
872	        if len(ids) < 6:
873	            continue  # Too few to worry about diversity
874	        # Count evidence per source URL
875	        source_counts: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
876	        for eid in ids:
877	            ev = ev_map_for_div.get(eid, {})
878	            src_url = ev.get("source_url", "unknown")
879	            source_counts.setdefault(src_url, []).append(eid)
880	        max_per_source = max(3, int(len(ids) * max_source_pct))
881	        trimmed_ids = []
882	        for src_url, src_ids in source_counts.items():
883	            if len(src_ids) > max_per_source:
884	                # Keep highest-relevance evidence from this source
885	                src_ids_sorted = sorted(
886	                    src_ids,
887	                    key=lambda eid: ev_map_for_div.get(eid, {}).get("relevance_score", 0),
888	                    reverse=True,
889	                )
890	                trimmed_ids.extend(src_ids_sorted[:max_per_source])
891	                diversity_trimmed += len(src_ids) - max_per_source
892	            else:
893	                trimmed_ids.extend(src_ids)
894	        section_evidence[si] = trimmed_ids
895	
896	    if diversity_trimmed > 0:
897	        logger.info(
898	            "[polaris graph] FIX-CITE-DIV-1: Trimmed %d over-represented "
899	            "source evidence across sections (max %.0f%% per source)",
900	            diversity_trimmed, max_source_pct * 100,
901	        )
902	
903	    # FIX-RISK-QUORUM: if a section's title or description contains risk-axis
904	    # terms, ensure it receives at least PG_RISK_QUORUM_MIN risk-axis evidence
905	    # pieces (pulled from the full pool, bypassing cluster routing). Without
906	    # this, a Jaccard word-overlap cluster-to-section map can starve a risk
907	    # section when clusters were themed around benefits.
908	    risk_axis_section_terms = (
909	        "risk", "adverse", "safety", "harm", "side effect",
910	        "side-effect", "contraindicat",
911	    )
912	    risk_axis_ev_categories = {
913	        "risk", "adverse_event", "contraindication", "safety",
914	    }
915	    risk_axis_ev_keywords = (
916	        "adverse", "contraindicat", "side effect", "side-effect",
917	        "hypoglyc", "disorder", "eating disorder", "bone density",
918	        "muscle loss", "lean body mass", "pregnancy", "pregnant",
919	        "adolescent", "teen", "mortality", "harm",
920	        "dizziness", "nausea", "irritab",
921	    )
922	    quorum_min = int(os.getenv("PG_RISK_QUORUM_MIN", "2"))
923	    quorum_added = 0
924	    for si, section in enumerate(sorted_sections):
925	        title_desc = f"{section.title} {section.description}".lower()
926	        if not any(t in title_desc for t in risk_axis_section_terms):
927	            continue
928	        existing_ids = set(section_evidence[si])
929	        existing_risk_count = 0
930	        for eid in existing_ids:
931	            ev = ev_map_for_div.get(eid, {})
932	            cat = (ev.get("fact_category", "") or "").lower()
933	            blob = (
934	                (ev.get("statement", "") or "")
935	                + " "
936	                + (ev.get("direct_quote", "") or "")
937	            ).lower()
938	            if cat in risk_axis_ev_categories or any(
939	                k in blob for k in risk_axis_ev_keywords
940	            ):
941	                existing_risk_count += 1

995	def _validate_outline_evidence(outline: ReportOutline) -> ReportOutline:
996	    """Deduplicate evidence assignments and ensure minimum per section.
997	
998	    1. Checks for evidence_ids appearing in more than one section.
999	       For any duplicates, keeps the evidence in the section where it
1000	       appears first (by section order) and removes from later sections.
1001	    2. FIX-QG1: Ensures every section has >= 3 evidence_ids by
1002	       redistributing from over-assigned sections.
1003	    """
1004	    min_evidence_per_section = int(
1005	        os.getenv("PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION", "3")
1006	    )
1007	
1008	    seen: set[str] = set()
1009	    dedup_count = 0
1010	
1011	    sorted_sections = sorted(outline.sections, key=lambda s: s.order)
1012	    for section in sorted_sections:
1013	        unique_ids = []
1014	        for eid in section.evidence_ids:
1015	            if eid in seen:
1016	                dedup_count += 1
1017	            else:
1018	                seen.add(eid)
1019	                unique_ids.append(eid)
1020	        section.evidence_ids = unique_ids
1021	
1022	    if dedup_count > 0:
1023	        logger.warning(
1024	            "[polaris graph] FIX-C1: Deduplicated %d cross-section evidence assignments",
1025	            dedup_count,
1026	        )
1027	
1028	    # FIX-QG1+STARVATION: Redistribute evidence to starved sections.
1029	    # Old: donor threshold >10 evidence. With 53 evidence / 8 sections = 6.6 avg,
1030	    # NO section had >10 → 3 sections got 0 evidence → empty sections.
1031	    # New: adaptive donor threshold = max(avg+2, min_evidence_per_section+1).
1032	    # Donors keep at least min_evidence_per_section, donate the rest.
1033	    starved = [s for s in sorted_sections if len(s.evidence_ids) < min_evidence_per_section]
1034	    if starved:
1035	        total_ev = sum(len(s.evidence_ids) for s in sorted_sections)
1036	        avg_ev = total_ev / max(len(sorted_sections), 1)
1037	        donor_threshold = max(int(avg_ev) + 2, min_evidence_per_section + 1)
1038	        keep_minimum = min_evidence_per_section  # Donors keep at least this many
1039	
1040	        donor_pool: list[str] = []
1041	        for section in sorted_sections:
1042	            if len(section.evidence_ids) > donor_threshold:
1043	                excess = section.evidence_ids[keep_minimum:]
1044	                section.evidence_ids = section.evidence_ids[:keep_minimum]
1045	                donor_pool.extend(excess)
1046	
1047	        # Distribute from donor pool to starved sections
1048	        redistributed = 0
1049	        for section in starved:
1050	            needed = min_evidence_per_section - len(section.evidence_ids)
1051	            while needed > 0 and donor_pool:
1052	                section.evidence_ids.append(donor_pool.pop(0))
1053	                needed -= 1
1054	                redistributed += 1
1055	
1056	        if redistributed > 0:
1057	            logger.info(
1058	                "[polaris graph] FIX-QG1: Redistributed %d evidence_ids to "
1059	                "%d starved sections (min=%d per section)",
1060	                redistributed,
1061	                len(starved),
1062	                min_evidence_per_section,
1063	            )
1064	
1065	        # Log remaining starved sections (couldn't fix)
1066	        still_starved = [
1067	            s for s in sorted_sections
1068	            if len(s.evidence_ids) < min_evidence_per_section
1069	        ]
1070	        if still_starved:
1071	            logger.warning(
1072	                "[polaris graph] FIX-QG1: %d sections still below %d evidence_ids "
1073	                "after redistribution: %s",
1074	                len(still_starved),
1075	                min_evidence_per_section,
1076	                [s.title[:40] for s in still_starved],
1077	            )
1078	
1079	    return outline

2856	def _format_cluster_detail(
2857	    clusters: list[dict], evidence: list[EvidencePiece]
2858	) -> str:
2859	    """FIX-QM27: Format clusters with evidence summaries for compact outline prompt.
2860	
2861	    Instead of listing all 600+ evidence pieces individually (~40K chars),
2862	    groups them by cluster with truncated statements (~5-8K chars total).
2863	    This keeps the outline prompt within the model's effective output budget.
2864	    """
2865	    # Build evidence lookup
2866	    ev_map: dict[str, EvidencePiece] = {
2867	        e.get("evidence_id", ""): e for e in evidence
2868	    }
2869	
2870	    parts = []
2871	    assigned_ids: set[str] = set()
2872	
2873	    # FIX-2: Cap per-cluster evidence display to keep outline prompt compact.
2874	    # With 15 clusters x 850 evidence, old limit of 40/cluster = 30K+ chars
2875	    # causing 3x timeout + fallback. Now configurable, default 10.
2876	    max_outline_ev = int(os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_EVIDENCE_PER_CLUSTER", "10"))
2877	
2878	    for i, c in enumerate(clusters, 1):
2879	        theme = c.get("theme", "Unknown")
2880	        desc = c.get("description", "")[:200]
2881	        ev_ids = c.get("evidence_ids", [])
2882	        strength = c.get("strength", "moderate")
2883	
2884	        parts.append(
2885	            f"## Cluster {i}: {theme} ({len(ev_ids)} evidence, strength={strength})"
2886	        )
2887	        parts.append(f"Description: {desc}")
2888	
2889	        # FIX-2: Show only top evidence per cluster, prioritizing GOLD/SILVER
2890	        # Sort evidence IDs by quality tier (GOLD first, then SILVER, then BRONZE)
2891	        tier_order = {"GOLD": 0, "SILVER": 1, "BRONZE": 2}
2892	        sorted_ev_ids = sorted(
2893	            ev_ids,
2894	            key=lambda eid: tier_order.get(
2895	                ev_map.get(eid, {}).get("quality_tier", "BRONZE"), 2
2896	            ),
2897	        )
2898	
2899	        shown = 0
2900	        for eid in sorted_ev_ids:
2901	            if shown >= max_outline_ev:
2902	                break
2903	            ev = ev_map.get(eid)
2904	            if ev:
2905	                quality = ev.get("quality_tier", "?")
2906	                stmt = ev.get("statement", "")[:120]
2907	                parts.append(f"  - [{eid}] ({quality}) {stmt}")
2908	                assigned_ids.add(eid)
2909	                shown += 1
2910	        if len(ev_ids) > max_outline_ev:
2911	            parts.append(f"  ... +{len(ev_ids) - shown} more evidence assigned to this cluster")
2912	            assigned_ids.update(ev_ids[max_outline_ev:])
2913	
2914	        parts.append("")
2915	
2916	    # Show unassigned evidence (not in any cluster) — also capped
2917	    max_unassigned_display = max_outline_ev  # FIX-2: Same cap for unassigned
2918	    unassigned = [
2919	        e for e in evidence
2920	        if e.get("evidence_id", "") not in assigned_ids
2921	    ]
2922	    if unassigned:
2923	        parts.append(f"## Unassigned evidence ({len(unassigned)} pieces)")
2924	        for e in unassigned[:max_unassigned_display]:
2925	            eid = e.get("evidence_id", "?")
2926	            quality = e.get("quality_tier", "?")
2927	            stmt = e.get("statement", "")[:120]
2928	            parts.append(f"  - [{eid}] ({quality}) {stmt}")
2929	        if len(unassigned) > max_unassigned_display:
2930	            parts.append(f"  ... +{len(unassigned) - max_unassigned_display} more")
2931	        parts.append("")
2932	
2933	    return "\n".join(parts)

2981	def _deduplicate_drafts(text: str) -> str:
2982	    """Detect and remove duplicate drafts in misrouted reasoning_content.
2983	
2984	    When reasoning_content is used as content, the LLM often writes
2985	    Draft 1 -> word counts -> Draft 2 (improved). Both drafts share
2986	    near-identical opening sentences. Keep only the LAST draft.
2987	    """
2988	    paragraphs = text.split("\n\n")
2989	    if len(paragraphs) < 4:
2990	        return text
2991	
2992	    # Extract first ~60 chars of each paragraph (normalized)
2993	    def _sig(para: str) -> str:
2994	        return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", para.strip()[:60]).lower()
2995	
2996	    # Find paragraphs with matching signatures
2997	    sigs: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
2998	    for i, p in enumerate(paragraphs):
2999	        sig = _sig(p)
3000	        if len(sig) < 30:
3001	            continue
3002	        if sig in sigs:
3003	            sigs[sig].append(i)
3004	        else:
3005	            sigs[sig] = [i]
3006	
3007	    # If any paragraph appears twice AND they're separated by other content,
3008	    # we likely have multiple drafts. Require the duplicates to be separated
3009	    # by at least 1 intervening paragraph.
3010	    dup_with_gap = False
3011	    for indices in sigs.values():
3012	        if len(indices) > 1 and (indices[-1] - indices[0]) >= 2:
3013	            dup_with_gap = True
3014	            break
3015	
3016	    if not dup_with_gap:
3017	        return text
3018	
3019	    # Find the boundary: last occurrence of the first duplicated paragraph
3020	    first_dup_sig = None
3021	    for sig, indices in sigs.items():
3022	        if len(indices) > 1:
3023	            first_dup_sig = sig
3024	            break
3025	
3026	    if first_dup_sig is None:
3027	        return text
3028	
3029	    last_start = sigs[first_dup_sig][-1]
3030	
3031	    # Keep everything from the last draft onwards
3032	    deduped = "\n\n".join(paragraphs[last_start:])
3033	    removed_words = len(text.split()) - len(deduped.split())
3034	
3035	    if removed_words > 0:
3036	        logger.info(
3037	            "[polaris graph] Draft dedup: removed %d words (earlier draft)",
3038	            removed_words,
3039	        )
3040	
3041	    return deduped

3044	def _scrub_cot(text: str) -> str:
3045	    """Remove chain-of-thought leakage from section content."""

3048	
3049	    # Step 1: Remove duplicate drafts (must come BEFORE line-level scrubbing)
3050	    cleaned = _deduplicate_drafts(cleaned)

3053	    # GLM-5 writes patterns like "ketone bodies—including Evidence suggests that β-hydroxybutyrate"
3054	    # where "Evidence suggests that" is inserted between a dash/comma and a continuation.
3055	    _esg_pattern = re.compile(
3056	        r'(\u2014|\u2013|-|,|;)(\s*(?:including\s+)?)Evidence suggests that\s+',
3057	        re.IGNORECASE,

3060	    if _esg_count:
3061	        cleaned = _esg_pattern.sub(r'\1\2', cleaned)
3062	    # Also handle: "[word] Evidence suggests that [lowercase word]" (no dash)

3064	        r'(\w)\s+Evidence suggests that\s+([a-z])',
3065	    )
3066	    _esg_count2 = len(_esg_pattern2.findall(cleaned))
3067	    if _esg_count2:
3068	        cleaned = _esg_pattern2.sub(r'\1 \2', cleaned)
3069	    _esg_total = _esg_count + _esg_count2
3070	    if _esg_total:
3071	        logger.info(
3072	            "[polaris graph] FIX-ESG: Removed %d broken 'Evidence suggests that' mid-sentence injections",
3073	            _esg_total,
3074	        )
3075	
3076	    # Step 2: Apply line-level CoT patterns
3077	    for pattern in _COT_LINE_PATTERNS:
3078	        cleaned = pattern.sub("", cleaned)
3079	
3080	    # Remove excessive blank lines created by scrubbing
3081	    cleaned = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", cleaned).strip()
3082

3089	            original_len,
3090	            scrubbed_len,
3091	        )
3092	        return text
3093	
3094	    if scrubbed_len < original_len:

3096	            "[polaris graph] CoT scrub: %d -> %d words (-%d)",
3097	            original_len,
3098	            scrubbed_len,
3099	            original_len - scrubbed_len,
3100	        )

3105	def _clean_artifacts(text: str, section_titles: list[str] | None = None) -> str:
3106	    """FIX-059-C: Remove LLM artifacts that leak through generation and post-processing.
3107	
3108	    Handles:
3109	    1. Orphan transition fragments (e.g. "Additionally,." left after citation cleanup)
3110	    2. [CROSS-REF:...] markers not resolved during assembly
3111	    3. Section [X]/[Y]/[Z] placeholder references
3112	    4. Title echo (section body starting with its own title)
3113	    5. Markdown headers from expansion output (H-06)
3114	    6. Double spaces and empty lines
3115	
3116	    Args:
3117	        text: The section or report text to clean.
3118	        section_titles: Optional list of section titles to detect title echo.
3119	
3120	    Returns:
3121	        Cleaned text with artifacts removed.
3122	    """
3123	    if not text or not text.strip():
3124	        return text
3125	
3126	    cleaned = text
3127	
3128	    # 1. Strip orphan transition fragments: "Additionally,." or "Moreover."
3129	    #    These arise when _inject_transitions() adds a transition word before
3130	    #    a sentence that is later stripped during citation orphan cleanup,
3131	    #    leaving the transition word dangling with only punctuation.
3132	    cleaned = re.sub(
3133	        r'\b(Additionally|Moreover|Furthermore|In addition|Consequently|'
3134	        r'Specifically|Notably|Importantly|Critically|Similarly|Likewise|'
3135	        r'Conversely)[,.]?\.\s*',
3136	        '',
3137	        cleaned,
3138	    )
3139	
3140	    # 2. Strip [CROSS-REF:...] markers
3141	    cleaned = re.sub(r'\[CROSS-REF:[^\]]*\]', '', cleaned)
3142	
3143	    # 3. Strip Section [X]/[Y]/[Z] placeholder references and clean up
3144	    #    resulting empty sentences
3145	    cleaned = re.sub(r'(?:Section|section)\s+\[[A-Z]\]', '', cleaned)
3146	    # Clean up sentences that became empty after stripping placeholders
3147	    # e.g. "As discussed in ." -> remove entire empty sentence
3148	    cleaned = re.sub(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+[.!?]', '', cleaned)
3149	
3150	    # 4. Remove title echo: if body starts with the exact section title
3151	    if section_titles:
3152	        for title in section_titles:
3153	            if not title:
3154	                continue
3155	            # Case-insensitive match at start of text (with optional markdown header)
3156	            pattern = re.compile(
3157	                r'^(?:#{1,4}\s+)?' + re.escape(title) + r'\s*\n?',
3158	                re.IGNORECASE,
3159	            )
3160	            cleaned = pattern.sub('', cleaned, count=1)
3161	
3162	    # 5. Strip markdown headers from expansion output (H-06)
3163	    #    Expansion LLM sometimes outputs "## Subsection Title" headers
3164	    cleaned = re.sub(r'^#{1,4}\s+.*$', '', cleaned, flags=re.MULTILINE)
3165	
3166	    # 6. Final cleanup: double spaces, excessive blank lines
3167	    cleaned = re.sub(r'  +', ' ', cleaned)
3168	    cleaned = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", cleaned)
3169	    cleaned = cleaned.strip()
3170	
3171	    if len(cleaned) < len(text.strip()):
3172	        logger.info(
3173	            "[polaris graph] FIX-059-C: _clean_artifacts() removed %d chars",
3174	            len(text.strip()) - len(cleaned),
3175	        )
3176	
3177	    return cleaned

3181	def _limit_hedging(text: str) -> str:
3182	    """FIX-R13: Post-processing enforcement of hedging word limits.
3183	
3184	    Prompt instructions alone don't reliably limit hedging. This function
3185	    enforces a hard cap by replacing excess hedging words with confident
3186	    alternatives.
3187	    """
3188	    if not text or not text.strip():
3189	        return text
3190	
3191	    max_hedging = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_HEDGING_PER_SECTION", "4"))
3192	
3193	    # Pattern matches common hedging words/phrases
3194	    _hedging_pattern = re.compile(
3195	        r'\b(may|might|potentially|possibly|could|perhaps|appears to|seems to)\b',
3196	        re.IGNORECASE,
3197	    )
3198	
3199	    matches = list(_hedging_pattern.finditer(text))
3200	    if len(matches) <= max_hedging:
3201	        return text
3202	
3203	    # Keep the first max_hedging instances, remove/replace the rest
3204	    excess = matches[max_hedging:]
3205	    # Process in reverse order to preserve string positions
3206	    result = text
3207	    removed = 0
3208	    for match in reversed(excess):
3209	        word = match.group(0).lower()
3210	        start, end = match.start(), match.end()
3211	
3212	        if word in ("potentially", "possibly", "perhaps"):
3213	            # Simply remove: "potentially harmful" -> "harmful"
3214	            # Also remove trailing space
3215	            if end < len(result) and result[end] == ' ':
3216	                result = result[:start] + result[end + 1:]
3217	            else:
3218	                result = result[:start] + result[end:]
3219	            removed += 1
3220	        elif word in ("may", "might"):
3221	            # "may cause" -> "causes" (simplified: just remove "may "/"might ")
3222	            if end < len(result) and result[end] == ' ':
3223	                result = result[:start] + result[end + 1:]
3224	            else:
3225	                result = result[:start] + result[end:]
3226	            removed += 1
3227	        elif word == "could":
3228	            # "could affect" -> "can affect"
3229	            result = result[:start] + "can" + result[end:]
3230	            removed += 1
3231	        elif word in ("appears to", "seems to"):
3232	            # Remove entirely
3233	            if end < len(result) and result[end] == ' ':
3234	                result = result[:start] + result[end + 1:]
3235	            else:
3236	                result = result[:start] + result[end:]
3237	            removed += 1
3238	
3239	    # Re-capitalize sentence starts that may have been affected
3240	    result = re.sub(r'(\. +)([a-z])', lambda m: m.group(1) + m.group(2).upper(), result)
3241	    # Clean double spaces
3242	    result = re.sub(r'  +', ' ', result)
3243	
3244	    if removed > 0:
3245	        logger.info(
3246	            "[polaris graph] FIX-R13: Removed/replaced %d/%d excess hedging words "
3247	            "(max %d per section)",
3248	            removed, len(matches), max_hedging,
3249	        )
3250	
3251	    return result

3254	def _limit_transitions(text: str) -> str:
3255	    """FIX-059-K: Limit transition word density to prevent over-saturation.
3256	
3257	    LLM writes transitions + post-processor adds more, causing 1 transition
3258	    every 81 words (129 in 10,500 words). This function enforces a cap of
3259	    ~60 per 10,000 words by removing transitions from middle paragraphs
3260	    when density exceeds 1 per 150 words.
3261	
3262	    Args:
3263	        text: Section content text.
3264	
3265	    Returns:
3266	        Text with transition density capped.
3267	    """
3268	    if not text or not text.strip():
3269	        return text
3270	
3271	    # LAW VI: Thresholds from environment
3272	    max_density = float(os.getenv("PG_TRANSITION_MAX_DENSITY", "150"))  # 1 per N words
3273	    cap_per_10k = int(os.getenv("PG_TRANSITION_CAP_PER_10K", "60"))
3274	
3275	    _transition_words = [
3276	        "Additionally", "Moreover", "Furthermore", "In addition",
3277	        "Consequently", "Specifically", "Notably", "Importantly",
3278	        "Similarly", "Likewise", "Conversely", "However",
3279	        "Nevertheless", "Nonetheless",
3280	    ]
3281	    _transition_pattern = re.compile(
3282	        r"\b(" + "|".join(_transition_words) + r")\b",
3283	        re.IGNORECASE,
3284	    )
3285	
3286	    word_count = len(text.split())
3287	    if word_count == 0:
3288	        return text
3289	
3290	    all_matches = list(_transition_pattern.finditer(text))
3291	    total_transitions = len(all_matches)
3292	
3293	    if total_transitions == 0:
3294	        return text
3295	
3296	    # Check if density exceeds threshold
3297	    density_words_per_transition = word_count / total_transitions
3298	    scaled_cap = max(1, int(cap_per_10k * word_count / 10000))
3299	
3300	    if density_words_per_transition >= max_density and total_transitions <= scaled_cap:
3301	        return text  # Already within limits
3302	
3303	    # Split into paragraphs; keep first and last paragraph intact
3304	    paragraphs = text.split("\n\n")
3305	    if len(paragraphs) <= 2:
3306	        return text  # Only first/last -- nothing to trim
3307	
3308	    target_removals = max(0, total_transitions - scaled_cap)
3309	    if target_removals == 0:
3310	        return text
3311	
3312	    removed = 0
3313	    result_paragraphs = []
3314	    for idx, para in enumerate(paragraphs):
3315	        # Keep first and last paragraphs unchanged
3316	        if idx == 0 or idx == len(paragraphs) - 1:
3317	            result_paragraphs.append(para)
3318	            continue
3319	
3320	        if removed >= target_removals:
3321	            result_paragraphs.append(para)
3322	            continue
3323	
3324	        # Remove transition words at sentence starts in middle paragraphs
3325	        def _strip_transition(match, _para=para):
3326	            nonlocal removed
3327	            if removed >= target_removals:
3328	                return match.group(0)
3329	            # Only strip if transition starts a sentence (preceded by '. ' or start-of-para)
3330	            start = match.start()
3331	            pre = _para[:start].rstrip()
3332	            if not pre or pre.endswith(".") or pre.endswith("?") or pre.endswith("!"):
3333	                removed += 1
3334	                return ""
3335	            return match.group(0)
3336	
3337	        cleaned_para = _transition_pattern.sub(_strip_transition, para)
3338	        # Clean up artifacts: leading comma/space, double spaces
3339	        cleaned_para = re.sub(r"^[,\s]+", "", cleaned_para)
3340	        cleaned_para = re.sub(r"(?<=\.)\s+[,]\s+", " ", cleaned_para)
3341	        cleaned_para = re.sub(r"  +", " ", cleaned_para)
3342	        # Re-capitalize first letter after removal if needed
3343	        if cleaned_para and cleaned_para[0].islower():
3344	            cleaned_para = cleaned_para[0].upper() + cleaned_para[1:]
3345	        result_paragraphs.append(cleaned_para)
3346	
3347	    result = "\n\n".join(result_paragraphs)
3348	
3349	    # FIX-R3: Capitalize sentence starts after transition removal
3350	    # Split by paragraph, check each paragraph's first char
3351	    _r3_paragraphs = result.split("\n\n")
3352	    _r3_fixed = []
3353	    for _r3_p in _r3_paragraphs:
3354	        _r3_p = _r3_p.strip()
3355	        if _r3_p and _r3_p[0].islower():
3356	            _r3_p = _r3_p[0].upper() + _r3_p[1:]
3357	        # Also fix sentence starts within paragraph (after ". ")
3358	        _r3_p = re.sub(r'(\.\s+)([a-z])', lambda m: m.group(1) + m.group(2).upper(), _r3_p)
3359	        _r3_fixed.append(_r3_p)
3360	    result = "\n\n".join(_r3_fixed)
3361	
3362	    if removed > 0:
3363	        logger.info(
3364	            "[polaris graph] FIX-059-K: Removed %d/%d transition words "
3365	            "(density: 1/%.0f words -> 1/%.0f words, cap: %d per 10K)",
3366	            removed,
3367	            total_transitions,
3368	            density_words_per_transition,
3369	            word_count / max(total_transitions - removed, 1),
3370	            scaled_cap,
3371	        )
3372	
3373	    return result

3376	def _break_long_paragraphs(text: str) -> str:
3377	    """FIX-059-L: Break excessively long paragraphs for readability.
3378	
3379	    Splits any paragraph exceeding 300 words at the nearest sentence boundary
3380	    after approximately 250 words. Uses regex-based sentence splitting to
3381	    avoid adding new dependencies.
3382	
3383	    Args:
3384	        text: Section content text.
3385	
3386	    Returns:
3387	        Text with long paragraphs broken into smaller ones.
3388	    """
3389	    if not text or not text.strip():
3390	        return text
3391	
3392	    # LAW VI: Threshold from environment
3393	    max_paragraph_words = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_PARAGRAPH_WORDS", "300"))
3394	    split_target_words = int(os.getenv("PG_PARAGRAPH_SPLIT_TARGET", "250"))
3395	
3396	    paragraphs = text.split("\n\n")
3397	    result_paragraphs = []
3398	    splits_made = 0
3399	
3400	    for para in paragraphs:
3401	        words = para.split()
3402	        if len(words) <= max_paragraph_words:
3403	            result_paragraphs.append(para)
3404	            continue
3405	
3406	        # Split at sentence boundaries using regex
3407	        # Handles abbreviations like U.S., Dr., Prof., e.g., i.e. by requiring
3408	        # the period to be followed by a space and an uppercase letter or [
3409	        sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z\[])', para)
3410	
3411	        if len(sentences) <= 1:
3412	            result_paragraphs.append(para)
3413	            continue
3414	
3415	        current_chunk: list[str] = []
3416	        current_word_count = 0
3417	
3418	        for sent in sentences:
3419	            sent_words = len(sent.split())
3420	            if current_word_count + sent_words > split_target_words and current_chunk:
3421	                result_paragraphs.append(" ".join(current_chunk))
3422	                current_chunk = [sent]
3423	                current_word_count = sent_words
3424	                splits_made += 1
3425	            else:
3426	                current_chunk.append(sent)
3427	                current_word_count += sent_words
3428	
3429	        if current_chunk:
3430	            result_paragraphs.append(" ".join(current_chunk))
3431	
3432	        # FIX-R5: Clause-level split for chunks still exceeding 300 words
3433	        # After sentence splitting, some chunks may still be too long
3434	        # (e.g., single very long sentences). Split at clause boundaries.
3435	        _r5_final = []
3436	        for _r5_chunk in result_paragraphs[len(result_paragraphs) - splits_made - 1:]:
3437	            if len(_r5_chunk.split()) > max_paragraph_words:
3438	                _r5_clauses = re.split(
3439	                    r'(?<=; )(?=[A-Z])|(?<=, which )|(?<=, and )(?=[A-Z])|(?<=, but )(?=[A-Z])',
3440	                    _r5_chunk,
3441	                )
3442	                if len(_r5_clauses) > 1:
3443	                    _r5_sub_chunk: list[str] = []
3444	                    _r5_sub_wc = 0
3445	                    for _r5_cl in _r5_clauses:
3446	                        _r5_cl_wc = len(_r5_cl.split())
3447	                        if _r5_sub_wc + _r5_cl_wc > split_target_words and _r5_sub_chunk:
3448	                            _r5_final.append("".join(_r5_sub_chunk))
3449	                            _r5_sub_chunk = [_r5_cl]
3450	                            _r5_sub_wc = _r5_cl_wc
3451	                            splits_made += 1
3452	                        else:
3453	                            _r5_sub_chunk.append(_r5_cl)
3454	                            _r5_sub_wc += _r5_cl_wc
3455	                    if _r5_sub_chunk:
3456	                        _r5_final.append("".join(_r5_sub_chunk))
3457	                else:
3458	                    _r5_final.append(_r5_chunk)
3459	            else:
3460	                _r5_final.append(_r5_chunk)
3461	        # Replace the tail of result_paragraphs with clause-split versions
3462	        if _r5_final:
3463	            _r5_start = len(result_paragraphs) - splits_made - 1
3464	            if _r5_start >= 0:
3465	                result_paragraphs = result_paragraphs[:_r5_start] + _r5_final
3466	
3467	
3468	    result = "\n\n".join(result_paragraphs)
3469	
3470	    if splits_made > 0:
3471	        logger.info(
3472	            "[polaris graph] FIX-059-L: Broke %d long paragraph(s) at sentence boundaries",
3473	            splits_made,
3474	        )
3475	
3476	    return result
3477	
3478	
3479	def _format_evidence_for_writing(evidence: list[EvidencePiece]) -> str:
3480	    """Format evidence for section writing (detailed).
3481	
3482	    FIX-5: Sorts evidence by tier (GOLD first) then relevance DESC so the
3483	    LLM sees the strongest evidence first and prioritizes it.
3484	    FIX-MP4: Adds [VERIFIED]/[UNVERIFIED] tags based on verification status.
3485	    FIX-MP16: Includes direct_quote to give LLM actual source text for
3486	    tighter claim-evidence coupling (ReClaim-lite approach).
3487	    """
3488	    # FIX-5: Sort by quality tier (GOLD=0, SILVER=1, BRONZE=2) then relevance DESC
3489	    _tier_rank = {"GOLD": 0, "SILVER": 1, "BRONZE": 2}
3490	    sorted_evidence = sorted(
3491	        evidence,
3492	        key=lambda e: (
3493	            _tier_rank.get(e.get("quality_tier", "BRONZE"), 2),
3494	            -(e.get("relevance_score", 0.0)),
3495	        ),
3496	    )
3497	
3498	    lines = []
3499	    _current_tier = None
3500	    for e in sorted_evidence:
3501	        # FIX-5: Add tier header when tier changes
3502	        _tier = e.get("quality_tier", "BRONZE")
3503	        if _tier != _current_tier:
3504	            _current_tier = _tier
3505	            lines.append(f"--- {_tier} EVIDENCE (sorted by relevance) ---")
3506	
3507	        # FIX-MP4: Verification status tag
3508	        verification_method = e.get("verification_method", "")
3509	        if verification_method == "api_error" or verification_method == "":
3510	            verify_tag = "[UNVERIFIED]"
3511	        elif e.get("is_faithful") is True:
3512	            verify_tag = "[VERIFIED]"
3513	        elif e.get("is_faithful") is False:
3514	            verify_tag = "[UNVERIFIED]"
3515	        else:
3516	            verify_tag = "[UNVERIFIED]"
3517	
3518	        # FIX-MP16: Include full direct_quote for source grounding
3519	        quote = e.get("direct_quote", "")
3520	        quote_line = f'  Direct quote: "{quote[:500]}"' if quote else ""
3521	
3522	        lines.append(
3523	            f"Evidence ID: {e.get('evidence_id', '?')} {verify_tag}\n"
3524	            f"  Statement: {e.get('statement', '')}\n"
3525	            f"{quote_line}\n"
3526	            f"  Source: {e.get('source_title', '')} ({e.get('year', '?')})\n"
3527	            f"  URL: {e.get('source_url', '')}\n"
3528	            f"  Quality: {e.get('quality_tier', '?')} | Relevance: {e.get('relevance_score', 0):.2f}\n"
3529	        )
3530	    return "\n".join(lines) if lines else "No evidence assigned to this section."

1	"""

5	diagrams, then generates content-level Mermaid.js code grounded in the
6	section text and supporting evidence. All diagram facts are traceable to

58	class DiagramRecommendation(BaseModel):
59	    """A single recommendation for a diagram to add to a report section."""
60	
61	    section_id: str = Field(description="The section_id this diagram targets")
62	    section_title: str = Field(description="Title of the targeted section")
63	    diagram_type: str = Field(description="One of the supported DIAGRAM_TYPES keys")

68	    @field_validator("diagram_type")
69	    @classmethod
70	    def validate_diagram_type(cls, v: str) -> str:
71	        """Coerce to closest valid type; reject unknowns."""
72	        normalised = v.strip().lower().replace("-", "_").replace(" ", "_")
73	        if normalised in DIAGRAM_TYPES:
74	            return normalised
75	        # Attempt fuzzy match on prefix
76	        for key in DIAGRAM_TYPES:
77	            if normalised.startswith(key[:6]):
78	                logger.debug(

97	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
98	# Mermaid validation helpers
99	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
100	
101	# Valid Mermaid diagram start tokens (non-exhaustive but covers common types)
102	_MERMAID_START_PATTERNS = [
103	    "graph ",
104	    "graph\n",
105	    "flowchart ",
106	    "flowchart\n",
107	    "sequenceDiagram",
108	    "classDiagram",
109	    "stateDiagram",
110	    "erDiagram",
111	    "gantt",
112	    "pie",
113	    "journey",
114	    "gitgraph",
115	    "mindmap",
116	    "timeline",
117	    "xychart",
118	    "block-beta",
119	    "sankey-beta",
120	    "quadrantChart",
121	]
122	
123	_CODE_FENCE_RE = re.compile(
124	    r"^```(?:mermaid|mmd|text)?\s*\n(.*?)```\s*$",
125	    re.DOTALL | re.MULTILINE,
126	)
127	
128	
129	def _strip_code_fences(raw: str) -> str:
130	    """Remove markdown code fences wrapping Mermaid code."""
131	    text = raw.strip()
132	    match = _CODE_FENCE_RE.search(text)
133	    if match:
134	        return match.group(1).strip()
135	    # Also handle single-line fence at start/end
136	    if text.startswith("```"):
137	        lines = text.split("\n")
138	        # Drop first line (```mermaid) and last line (```)
139	        inner_lines = []
140	        started = False
141	        for line in lines:
142	            if not started and line.strip().startswith("```"):
143	                started = True
144	                continue
145	            if started and line.strip() == "```":
146	                break
147	            if started:
148	                inner_lines.append(line)
149	        if inner_lines:
150	            return "\n".join(inner_lines).strip()
151	    return text
152	
153	
154	def _validate_mermaid(code: str) -> bool:
155	    """
156	    Basic structural validation for Mermaid.js code.
157	
158	    Checks that the code begins with a recognised diagram declaration
159	    and contains at least one relationship or node definition. This is
160	    intentionally lenient — full rendering validation is left to the
161	    client-side Mermaid renderer.
162	    """
163	    if not code or len(code.strip()) < 10:
164	        return False
165	
166	    normalised = code.strip().lower()
167	    has_valid_start = any(
168	        normalised.startswith(pattern.lower()) for pattern in _MERMAID_START_PATTERNS
169	    )
170	    if not has_valid_start:
171	        logger.debug(
172	            "[smart_art] Mermaid validation failed: unrecognised start token. "
173	            "First 60 chars: '%s'",
174	            code.strip()[:60],
175	        )
176	        return False
177	
178	    # Must have at least two non-empty lines (declaration + content)
179	    meaningful_lines = [
180	        line for line in code.strip().split("\n") if line.strip()
181	    ]
182	    if len(meaningful_lines) < 2:
183	        logger.debug("[smart_art] Mermaid validation failed: fewer than 2 lines")
184	        return False
185	
186	    return True

196	    "and select the appropriate diagram type for each.\n\n"

202	    '"diagram_type": "process_flow", "description": "Step-by-step process of how compound X '

204	)

243	class SmartArtGenerator:
244	    """
245	    Generates content-level Mermaid.js diagrams for research report sections.
246	
247	    Usage:
248	        generator = SmartArtGenerator()
249	        diagrams = await generator.generate_smart_art_for_report(
250	            sections=report_sections,

252	            llm_client=openrouter_client,
253	        )
254	        # diagrams: {section_id: mermaid_code_string, ...}
255	    """
256	
257	    def __init__(self) -> None:
258	        self._tracer = get_tracer()
259	
260	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
261	    # Public API
262	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
263	
264	    async def analyze_sections(
265	        self,
266	        sections: list[dict],
267	        llm_client: OpenRouterClient,
268	    ) -> list[dict]:
269	        """
270	        Identify which sections (2-5) would benefit from a visual diagram.
271	
272	        Parameters
273	        ----------
274	        sections : list[dict]
275	            Report sections, each with at minimum ``section_id``, ``title``,
276	            and ``content`` keys (matching ``ReportSection`` TypedDict).
277	        llm_client : OpenRouterClient
278	            The shared LLM client instance.
279	
280	        Returns
281	        -------
282	        list[dict]
283	            Each entry: ``{"section_id", "section_title", "diagram_type",
284	            "description"}``. Empty list on total failure.
285	        """
286	        if not sections:
287	            logger.warning("[smart_art] analyze_sections called with empty sections list")
288	            return []
289	
290	        sections_text = self._format_sections_for_analysis(sections)
291	        prompt = _ANALYSIS_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format(
292	            sections_text=sections_text,
293	            max_diagrams=PG_MAX_SMART_ART,
294	        )
295	
296	        try:
297	            result: DiagramAnalysisResult = await llm_client.generate_structured(
298	                prompt=prompt,
299	                schema=DiagramAnalysisResult,
300	                system=_ANALYSIS_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
301	                max_tokens=_ANALYSIS_MAX_TOKENS,
302	                timeout=_ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT,
303	            )
304	        except Exception as exc:
305	            logger.error(
306	                "[smart_art] LLM analysis call failed: %s", str(exc)[:200]
307	            )
308	            return []
309	
310	        # Validate that recommended section_ids actually exist
311	        valid_ids = {s.get("section_id") or s.get("id", "") for s in sections}
312	        validated: list[dict] = []
313	        for rec in result.recommendations:
314	            if rec.section_id not in valid_ids:
315	                logger.warning(
316	                    "[smart_art] LLM recommended non-existent section_id '%s', skipping",
317	                    rec.section_id,
318	                )
319	                continue
320	            validated.append({
321	                "section_id": rec.section_id,
322	                "section_title": rec.section_title,
323	                "diagram_type": rec.diagram_type,
324	                "description": rec.description,
325	            })
326	
327	        # Enforce cap
328	        if len(validated) > PG_MAX_SMART_ART:
329	            logger.info(
330	                "[smart_art] Capping recommendations from %d to %d",
331	                len(validated),
332	                PG_MAX_SMART_ART,
333	            )
334	            validated = validated[:PG_MAX_SMART_ART]
335	
336	        logger.info(
337	            "[smart_art] Analysis complete: %d sections recommended for diagrams",
338	            len(validated),
339	        )
340	        return validated
